title: lasianthus africanus subsp. biokoensis
intro: Lasianthus africanus subsp. biokoensis, also known as a species of shrub in the family Rubiaceae, is a plant native to Bioko Island in Equatorial Guinea. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, with studies showing its extracts have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Lasianthus africanus subsp. biokoensis include its potential use in treating various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its leaves in water, which can be used as a tea for its medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
